assertions.evidence: Report CLAIMS 'Steps 1-5 skipped' in header, but INCLUDES: Application Security Profile, Data Classification (5 items), Compliance Matrix, Threat Register (5 threats), Domain Scores (SPS 4.3), Verdict, Remediation Roadmap. Agent recognized edge case but did NOT execute it — ran a full lightweight audit instead.

assertions.text: Policy follows Policies section format (Scope, Classification, Requirements, Exceptions, Review cycle, Effective date)
assertions.evidence: Lines 70-72: Scope, Classification, Effective date, Review cycle present. Lines 268: Exceptions. Requirements embedded in numbered playbook steps.

assertions.text: Policy uses provided context (KangNam Dev, data types, team size)
assertions.evidence: Line 70: 'KangNam Dev 조직', '1인 개발자 환경에 최적화', references '개발 환경 설정', 'API 키 참조'
4 detailed playbooks (A: API키 노출, B: SSH키 탈취, C: 동기화 무결성, D: GitHub 장애) with numbered steps, specific commands, and time targets

assertions.text: No SPS score or Domain Scores present
assertions.evidence: Domain Scores table present at lines 39-47 with SPS 4.3/10 and Verdict 'AT RISK'. These should not exist in a policy-only output.

without_agent.notes: Baseline correctly interpreted the request as policy-only — wrote a pure incident response playbook with NO security assessment. More comprehensive playbook: 10 sections, 6 incident scenarios (vs 4), asset inventory, detection methods table, recovery procedures, preventive controls checklist, post-incident documentation template. Better aligned with user intent.
